title: ai-badger agent scaffolding descriptor
description: Declarative specification of what files an agent needs scaffolded into a target project. Lives at features/<agent>/scaffolding.json. Read by scaffold.py during welcome-ai-badger and den-refresh.

agent.description: Agent name matching the features/<agent>/ directory and config.agents entries.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this scaffolding provides.

source.description: Path relative to the feature directory (features/<agent>/). For template entries, the .tmpl file to render.

target.description: Path in the scaffolded project (relative to project root).

managed.description: True = framework overwrites on re-scaffold. False = seed-once, project-owned after first scaffold.

seedOnce.description: True = written only on first scaffold, preserved on re-scaffold. Overrides managed when true. When combined with alsoTarget, both copies are skipped together.

template.description: True = render source as a template with standard slots (PROJECT_NAME, INVARIANTS, COMMANDS, etc.). False = copy source verbatim.

alsoTarget.description: Optional second target path to write the same content to (e.g. .hermes.md alias for HERMES.md).

aibCopy.description: Optional path under .ai-badger/ to write the source-of-truth copy (before the managed-header copy to target).

instructionsScoped.description: If true, write one copy per instruction file from the project's instruction set (copilot's .github/instructions/ pattern).
